title: Isolation of DNA from spots of old microscopic glass slides with mini column isolation kit for molecular analysis
author: Sudhir Bhatia
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Aim: Many institutes have a number of old fixed slides for various pathogens. Such slides can serve as the source of DNA, which can be used a reference material as well as prevalence studies of a pathogen. But there is no standard and reliable method in the literature how to isolate the DNA from such fixed spots as well as smears. Therefore, we decided to develop and standardize the method for DNA isolation from such spots and smears of glass slides. 

 Methods: Two different pathogens like  Anaplasma phytocytophilum  and  Echnicoccus  slides were used. The successful isolations of these pathogens were done with mini column kit. The confirmation of the successful isolations was achieved with pathogen specific real time and conventional PCR assays. Isolated DNA was stored at -20 0 C for more than 3 years. 

 Conclusion: We have demonstrated a robust and reproducible DNA isolation method from spots of fixed glass slides for two pathogens as examples. This method opens the new opportunities for conducting the molecular analysis of different pathogens like Mycobacterium, blood parasite like Plasmodium as well as for forensic molecular analysis in different laboratories.

The confirmation of the successful isolations was achieved with pathogen specific real time and conventional PCR assays. Isolated DNA was stored at -20 0 C for more than 3 years.

This method opens the new opportunities for conducting the molecular analysis of different pathogens like Mycobacterium, blood parasite like Plasmodium as well as for forensic molecular analysis in different laboratories.
